description_long: This guard rail consists of two thick, horizontal wooden beams supported by three sturdy vertical wooden posts. The wood has a weathered, dark brown finish, with visible grain and texture, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The posts are square in cross-section and evenly spaced, providing structural stability. The ends of the rails are blunt and squared off, with no additional end treatments or safety features. There are no visible fasteners, reflective markings, or metal reinforcements, giving it a simple, rustic appearance. This type of guard rail is commonly found in parks, along pathways, or as a boundary in suburban or rural urban settings.
description: A rustic, dark brown wooden guard rail with two horizontal beams and three square vertical posts, featuring a weathered texture and blunt ends.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the depth and thickness of the posts and rails, highlighting the wood grain and the spacing between posts.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the guard rail, the alignment of the horizontal beams, and the even spacing of the three posts.
description_view_2: Angled view similar to view 0, emphasizing the parallel arrangement of the rails and the robust construction of the posts.
description_view_3: Direct side view mirroring view 1, clearly showing the two horizontal rails and three supporting posts.
